branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from The Dome & Beyond
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Blue Jays pride
branding.idle_msg: No Blue Jays game in progress

columnists.name: Archie Ouellette
columnists.backstory: A Mississauga reporter who's covered the Jays since the '93 parade and still carries his press pass from Joe Carter's walk-off.
columnists.voice_sample: Guerrero drove in three, Gausman pitched into the seventh, and the Jays took two of three from Tampa. Record now sits at .500.

columnists.name: Margot Tremblay
columnists.backstory: A Leslieville bookseller who thinks every Jays team is one hot June away from the pennant and has said so every April for twenty years.
columnists.voice_sample: Bo's locked in, Kirk is framing like Pudge, and the Dome is going to shake like '93 before the leaves turn. Book it.

columnists.name: Doug Purvis
columnists.backstory: A retired Scarborough machinist who has only forgiven the 1994 strike on odd-numbered weekdays.
columnists.voice_sample: Rogers sold us out for a cable bill and a photo op. Every year it's the same sermon — and the pews are getting thin.
